message: Docs add package documentation and contributor guide

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+## Project Scope
+
+- `bun-workflow` is a Bun-first durable workflow engine.
+- Runtime and tests use Bun APIs, including `bun:sqlite`, `Bun.serve`, and `bun test`.
+- Keep runtime dependencies at zero unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.
+
+## Setup & Tooling
+
+- Use Bun 1.3.10.
+- Install dependencies with `bun install`.
+- Type checking uses `tsgo`.
+- Formatting uses `oxfmt`.
+- Linting uses `oxlint`.
+- Build output is produced with `bun run build`.
+
+## Commands
+
+- `bun run check` runs formatting checks, linting, and `tsgo`.
+- `bun test` runs the full test suite.
+- `bun run test:e2e` runs the `tests/` end-to-end suite.
+- `bun run build` builds the package into `dist/`.
+- `bun run release <patch|minor|major> [--dry]` bumps, commits, tags, and pushes a release.
+
+## Workflow Engine Guidelines
+
+- Preserve the public API shape around `createWorkflow`, `createEvent`, and `createRuntime`.
+- Durable boundaries must stay explicit: `step.run`, `step.sleep`, `step.sleepUntil`, and `step.waitForEvent`.
+- Keep SQLite write transactions short.
+- Prefer replay and checkpoints over in-memory progress tracking.
+- Respect the single authoritative worker model per SQLite database.
+- When adding tests under `tests/`, only exercise exported library APIs there. Internal modules can have unit tests closer to their source.
+
+## Code Style
+
+- TypeScript + ESM throughout.
+- Avoid default exports.
+- Prefer ASCII unless a file already requires Unicode.
+- Add comments only when they clarify non-obvious behavior.
+- Keep README examples realistic and runnable.
+
+## Testing
+
+- Add unit tests next to implementation files when it helps isolate logic.
+- Keep integration and end-to-end coverage in `tests/` using real SQLite files.
+- Do not replace SQLite with mocks in e2e coverage.
+- Mock timers only when a time-dependent API cannot be covered realistically otherwise.
+
+## Commits
+
+- Do atomic and small commits for logical changes, only when asked.
+- Capitalize the first letter of the commit message.
+- Prefix the commit with an area when it helps, for example `runtime`, `storage`, `docs`, or `ci`.
+
+## Release Workflow
+
+- Release tags use the form `vX.Y.Z`.
+- The release script should verify a clean working tree and the `main` branch before tagging.
+- GitHub Actions publishes to npm from pushed tags.
